• One correct mathematical process even if the answer is incorrect • Two further correct mathematical process even if the answer is incorrect • Two further correct mathematical processes even if the answers are incorrect • Final process and correct (1) (1) (1) (1) This calculation involves six mathematical processes 1) Calculation of molecular mass of N-phenylethanamide 2) Calculation of molecular mass of benzene 3) Divide by 135 (molecular mass of N-phenylethanamide) 4 Multiply by 78 (molecular mass of benzene) 5 Divide by 0.879 (density of benzene) 6) Divide by 35.2 × 100 (percentage yield) It is possible to carry out these processes in any order. Look first for the answer. Correct answer with some working scores (4). Next look for the processes and mark as shown in the Answer column. Two examples of calculation Route 1 M r of N-phenylethanamide = (12 x 8) + 9 + 14 + 16 = 135 (g mol ‒ ) M r of benzene = (12 × 6) + 6 = 78 (g mol ‒ ) moles of N-phenylethanamide = (10 ÷ 135) = 0.074074 (mol) mass of benzene for 100% yield = 0.074074 × 78 = 5.7778 / 5.78 (g) volume of for 100% yield = 5.7778 ÷ 0.879 = 6.573 (cm ) minimum volume = 6.573 ÷ 35.2 × 100 = 18.674 / 18.7 / 19 (cm ) Route 2 M r of N-phenylethanamide = (12 x 8) + 9 + 14 + 16 = 135 (g mol ‒ ) M r of benzene = (12 × 6) + 6 = 78 (g mol ‒ ) mass of N-phenylethanamide giving actual yield = 10 ÷ 35.2 × 100 = 28.409 (g) moles of N-phenylethanamide = 28.409 ÷ 135 = 0.21044 (mol) mass of benzene required = 0.21044 × 78 = 16.414 (g) volume of benzene required = 16.414 ÷ 0.879 = 18.674 / 18.7 / 19 (cm ) If no other mark is awarded allow (1) for 10 ÷ 135 = 0.074074 (mol) Allow TE throughout Ignore SF except 1SF (4) (Total for Question 14 = 19 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark
